title: Polymath tool surface
description: Validates a `tools/<name>/tool.json` manifest. A tool is a first-class sub-plugin unit — added like a skill (drop a folder), discoverable and dispatchable through the SAME surface registry. It is a thin, declarative pointer to an external capability action (an MCP tool, CLI, or HTTP call) rather than a full procedure. Add a `routing.yaml` next to tool.json (validated by surface-routing.schema.json) to make the tool deterministically routable; tools/build-surface-index.py compiles it into route-signals.json as a `kind: tool` surface. This replaces the per-connector bash 'detect-and-hint' scripts: the detection signals move into routing.yaml, and the tool/MCP detail lives here.

name.description: Tool id, lower-kebab/snake. Must match the containing directory name.

summary.description: One line: what the tool does and when to reach for it.

capability.description: Capability this tool serves (links to the binding model). Optional.

provider.description: Provider id this tool belongs to (matches a bindings/<provider>/). Optional.

transport.description: How the tool is invoked. Mirrors binding.schema.json transport.

mcpTools.description: Concrete MCP tool names this surface points the agent at (e.g. jira_get_issue). The deterministic value the old detect-*.sh hooks used to echo.

references.description: references/*.md docs with the full tool catalog for this provider.
